content.text: All 3 pods are Running, but the task is about ES cluster membership. Let me check actual ES cluster state.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d); A=""; [ -n "$PW" ] && A="-u elastic:$PW"
echo "=== HEALTH ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk $A --max-time 8 "http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cluster/health?pretty"
echo "=== NODES ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk $A --max-time 8 "http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/nodes?v"
echo "=== APP-DATA ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk $A --max-time 8 "http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/app-data/_count?pretty"
input.description: Check ES cluster health, nodes, app-data

content.text: Cluster is on HTTPS. Health is green with 3 nodes. Let me check node details and the statefulset spec to understand intended membership.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d)
echo "=== NODES ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk -u elastic:$PW --max-time 8 "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/nodes?v&h=name,node.role,master,version,ip"
echo "=== APP-DATA ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk -u elastic:$PW --max-time 8 "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/app-data/_count?pretty"
echo "=== STS replicas ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}'; echo
echo "=== STS pod template env (discovery) ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o yaml | grep -iE "replicas|discovery|initial_master|seed_hosts|minimum_master|cluster.name|node.name" | head -40

content.text: All 3 nodes are present and green now, but `app-data` index does not exist (the precondition seed used `http://` against an HTTPS server and silently failed). Let me examine the discovery config and cluster details to understand the actual injected drift.

input.command:
echo "=== es-config configmap ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch get cm es-config -o jsonpath='{.data.elasticsearch\.yml}'
echo; echo "=== voting config / master ==="; PW=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d)
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk -u elastic:$PW --max-time 8 "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cluster/state/metadata?filter_path=metadata.cluster_coordination&pretty"
echo "=== all indices ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk -u elastic:$PW --max-time 8 "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_cat/indices?v"
